column_rename_map.Formula Required to check if 2nd part of: Formula Required to check if 2nd part of Outlet Name does not equal City

spec.reasoning: The instruction references 'Store Name' as a column header and within the task logic ('2nd part of Store Name'). Since 'Store Name' is used as a column reference (not a general English phrase in the instruction body — the instruction says 'store names' generically but the column header is 'Store Name'), I rename it to 'Outlet Name' which is semantically equivalent and at the same descriptiveness level. The column C header is updated accordingly. 'City' appears both as a column name and as a common English word used extensively in the instruction ('city is mentioned', 'city name'), so per Rule 6 I do NOT rename it. Sheet1 is renamed to WorkSheet1. A distractor column 'Region' is added after the last column — it's plausibly related to geographic data (like City) but clearly different. I avoid blank_row_insert because the instruction references 'column C (not including title)' which assumes title is in row 1, and a blank row would shift that and cause ambiguity (as noted in the failed attempt). instruction_paraphrase is set to false per the rules.
